differentials.name: Pineal calcification Age:  no calcification < 5 years of age, in 8-10% at 8-14 years of age, in 40% by 20 years of age; in ⅔ of adult population
differentials.raw: Pineal calcification Age:  no calcification < 5 years of age, in 8-10% at 8-14 years of age, in 40% by 20 years of age; in ⅔ of adult population

differentials.name: Choroid plexus may calcify in all ventricles: most commonly in glomus within atrium of lateral ventricles, near foramen of Monro, tela choroidea of 3rd ventricle, roof of 4th ventricle, along foramina of Luschka
differentials.raw: Choroid plexus may calcify in all ventricles: most commonly in glomus within atrium of lateral ventricles, near foramen of Monro, tela choroidea of 3rd ventricle, roof of 4th ventricle, along foramina of Luschka

differentials.name: Dura, falx cerebri, falx cerebelli, tentorium
differentials.raw: Dura, falx cerebri, falx cerebelli, tentorium

differentials.name: Petroclinoid ligament
differentials.raw: Petroclinoid ligament (= reflection of tentorium between tip of dorsum sellae and apex of petrous bone)

differentials.name: Arteriosclerosis: particularly intracavernous segment of ICA, basilar a., vertebral a.
differentials.raw: Arteriosclerosis: particularly intracavernous segment of ICA, basilar a., vertebral a.

context: √  amorphous / ringlike calcification < 3 mm from midline usually < 10 mm in diameter √  ~ 30 mm above highest posterior elevation of pyramids CAVE: pineal calcification > 14 mm suggests pineal neoplasm (teratoma / pinealoma) Frequency:   ~ in ⅓ of population Age:  > 10 years of age √  posteriorly open C-shaped calcification 4-6 mm anterior to pineal gland Age:  > 3 years of age √  20-30 mm behind + slightly below pineal on lateral projection, symmetrical on AP projection DDx:  neurofibromatosis Frequency:   10% of population Age:  > 3 years of age DDx:  basal cell nevus syndrome (Gorlin syndr
